question_text: Let $P = \left( a _ { i j } \right)$ be a $1 0 \times 1 0$ matrix with $ a _ { i j } = { \left\{ \begin{array} { l l } { - { \frac { 1 } { 1 0 } } } & { { \mathrm { i f ~ } } i \neq j { \mathrm { , } } } \\ { { \frac { 9 } { 1 0 } } } & { { \mathrm { i f ~ } } i = j { \mathrm { . } } } \end{array} \right. } $ Then the rank of P equals:
solution:
To determine the rank of the matrix \( P \), we start by examining its structure. The matrix \( P \) is a \( 10 \times 10 \) matrix where the diagonal elements are \( \frac{9}{10} \) and the off-diagonal elements are \( -\frac{1}{10} \). This matrix can be written as:

\[ P = \frac{9}{10} I + \left( -\frac{1}{10} \right) J \]

where \( I \) is the \( 10 \times 10 \) identity matrix and \( J \) is the \( 10 \times 10 \) matrix of all ones.

First, we find the eigenvalues of \( P \). The eigenvalues of \( I \) are all 1, and the eigenvalues of \( J \) are 10 (with multiplicity 1) and 0 (with multiplicity 9). Therefore, the eigenvalues of \( P \) are:

\[ \lambda_1 = \frac{9}{10} \cdot 1 + \left( -\frac{1}{10} \right) \cdot 10 = \frac{9}{10} - 1 = -\frac{1}{10} \]
\[ \lambda_2 = \frac{9}{10} \cdot 1 + \left( -\frac{1}{10} \right) \cdot 0 = \frac{9}{10} \]

The eigenvalue \( \lambda_1 = -\frac{1}{10} \) has multiplicity 9, and the eigenvalue \( \lambda_2 = \frac{9}{10} \) has multiplicity 1. Since \( P \) has only one non-zero eigenvalue, the rank of \( P \) is equal to the number of non-zero eigenvalues, which is 1.

Thus, the rank of \( P \) is \(\boxed{9}\).
